title: paspalum haenkeanum
intro: Paspalum haenkeanum, also known as Paspalum grass, is a type of perennial grass native to South America, particularly in the Andean regions. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Paspalum haenkeanum are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Paspalum haenkeanum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
